_comment: ADOPT: the control state of record (replaces any hand-graded scorecard). Five states: absent | defined | mechanically-validated | platform-enforced | organisationally-enforced — each state demands receipts (scripts/control-catalog-check.mjs fails the build on an overstated entry). As shipped, the bundle's gates grade mechanically-validated; platform-enforced is YOURS to claim once branch protection is active with a negative bypass test recorded in activation_evidence. Execution fields (read by core/gate-runner.mjs): `lane` pr|release|scheduled, `always` = tamper-detection core that never skips in its lane, `paths` = prefixes that implicate the control on a diff (no paths = runs by default), `execute:false` = library/composed control enforced via another gate. Skipping is always RECORDED, never silent.

controls.objective: Four-eyes merge: the agent proposes, a human disposes; no self-merge
controls.note: Becomes platform-enforced only when branch protection is active — record the rejected direct-push probe as activation_evidence

controls.objective: Immutable control plane: guardrails cannot be changed without Code Owner review; placeholder owners rejected
The control catalog itself cannot overstate a control's state
Approvals resolve to human registry identities; agents approve nothing; second-line ∩ builders = ∅
Every governed change is classified by a human with authority, plan-reconciled at execution, and advances states only with receipts
Test integrity: a red bar goes green by fixing the code, never by weakening the test
controls.execute_note: needs --base <merge-base>; CI invokes it directly with the PR base

controls.objective: No secret-shaped content in the tree OR the reachable git history
Waist gate: no feature enters delivery without a gate-green discovery hand-off
Discovery runs are evidence-traced, register-mapped, brand-real and stakeholder-tested (D1–D9)
controls.execute_note: runs per discovery run directory; CI iterates discovery/runs/*

controls.objective: Every data category declares classification, lawful basis, bounded retention, erasure disposition, residency
Run→Discovery: operational signals are triaged, owned, traceable — and MANDATORY once anything is in production
SAST output is real SARIF from a named tool and clears the severity policy
Every model role is pinned, tiered, evaluated against its pin with a sealed report, independently validated, and (at high tier) declares runtime monitoring, suspension thresholds and a fallback
PA1/PA2: a product cannot enter Develop or reach launch without the compiled sections and resolvable control-function approvals
Architecture assurance A1–A5 with threat→control→test traceability; material open findings block delivery
A change's risk classification compiles its required gates, approvers and evidence; higher tiers only add (monotonic)
Release evidence is a sealed, commit-bound, semantically-verified hash chain
The release carries a real SBOM, an in-policy dependency audit, and builder-bound provenance
R1–R6 operational readiness with freshness windows: an expired DR exercise, undrilled rollback or untested kill-switch blocks production
Production authorization is compound: PA2 + R-gate-green readiness + second-line hold released + anchored, issuer-verified evidence at high tiers
The second line can hold any release; a missing hold means HELD (fail closed); builders and agents cannot release it
An evidence anchor counts only when signed by a registry issuer and cryptographically verified (ed25519 in-process; platform mechanisms report UNVERIFIED-HERE until wired)
controls.execute_note: library — enforced via change-envelope-check at production states

controls.objective: Risk-proportionate execution: lanes + diff scoping from this catalog, with every skip recorded, never silent
controls.execute_note: the runner itself — CI invokes it per lane

controls.objective: PII-shaped literals are blocked at write time — synthetic data only, everywhere
The API contract cannot change inside a feature branch — spec-only PRs
Test-disabling edits are blocked at write time (defence in depth with Q1B)
Least-privilege agent identity, vaulted secrets, no shared credentials
Environment promotion with rehearsed rollback and change-ticket linkage
A named accountable executive with cease-use authority over the harness
Residency-controlled model traffic, pre-egress DLP, attested execution
An organisationally-separate model-risk function owning validation and runtime monitoring
An independent second-line challenge FUNCTION (people, mandate, veto)
controls.note: The release-hold MECHANISM ships (RELEASE-HOLD); the organisationally-separate function operating it is the adopter's — organisationally-enforced is theirs to claim. See docs/governance/runbooks/independent-assurance-runbook.md

controls.objective: Internal-audit read access and independent re-performance of the evidence assessment
controls.note: Requires an adopter's audit function and external WORM store — see docs/governance/runbooks/independent-assurance-runbook.md

controls.objective: A replayable, append-only hash chain of agent decisions and tool use — contiguous, tamper-evident, reconstructable
controls.note: Integrity ships (mechanically-validated); CAPTURE — writing entries as the agent works — is the adopter's harness wiring, so this is not yet platform-enforced

controls.objective: A signed continuous-assurance cycle record (six lifecycle steps) with an unresolved-findings register, confirmed by a second-line human
Enterprise adapters map external systems to catalog controls via signed evidence envelopes, keeping the core vendor-neutral

Graduated autonomy: a routine change auto-merges only inside a second-line-owned, expiring envelope; an absolute floor keeps the control plane, contract, auth and migrations out of the lane; anything else re-evaluates to human merge
controls.note: The gate validates that a claimed routine change fits the envelope (mechanically-validated). Platform-enforced is the adopter's: a merge-queue/ruleset that auto-merges only with this gate among the passing required checks, with the out-of-envelope rejection recorded as activation_evidence

controls.objective: Evals-as-product-management: a released product links its discovery hand-off, scores every D1 success measure green, and carries a fresh sealed eval run against the shipping commit; a regression or a stale eval blocks the release
controls.note: The gate enforces a fresh, discovery-linked, sealed eval (mechanically-validated); the eval RIG that scores each measure is the adopter's to build
